requirement: the format specification admits a single interpretation

findings.observation: BCP 14 keywords are defined once and used in all-caps only with normative force, per the RFC 2119/8174 convention; obligation strength is explicit (MUST/MUST NOT/SHOULD/MAY) throughout.
Quantifiers are bounded ('at least two Rating Levels', 'exactly one assessment'), so rules do not lean on vague quantifiers without a stated bound.
Permissive lowercase 'can'/'may' phrasing is used for non-normative latitude and is distinguishable from normative all-caps keywords; each rule reads one way.

rationale: Each rule admits a single reading: obligation strength is explicit, BCP 14 keywords are used consistently, and quantifiers are bounded. Satisfies the requirement.
